question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to their own account ID before the pinger starts, given that loadConfig() reads destination from environment with no ownership or allowlist validation, causing all periodic HBAR transfers intended to fund the staking reward pool (0.0.98) to be redirected to the attacker's account, thereby stealing the HBAR that would have accrued as unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative integer via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR, given that loadConfig() only validates amountTinybar != 0 (line 111) and does not reject negative values, causing submitWithRetry() to call AddHbarTransfer(operatorID, +N) and AddHbarTransfer(0.0.98, -N), which reverses the transfer direction and drains HBAR from the staking reward account 0.0.98 into the operator account, constituting theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded default operatorKey '302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137' in loadConfig() (config.go line 40), given that this well-known genesis private key is publicly visible in the repository, to sign their own CryptoTransfer transactions as operator 0.0.2 and redirect staking reward pool transfers to their own account, stealing unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user control the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST environment variable to point to an attacker-controlled HTTP server, given that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() fetches node topology from cfg.mirrorRest with no TLS certificate validation or URL allowlist, causing the pinger to submit all HBAR transfers (including staking reward pool funding) to attacker-controlled consensus nodes that can manipulate or intercept the transactions, resulting in theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a crafted JSON response from a rogue mirror node REST endpoint, given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() parses nodesEnvelope without validating node_account_id values or service_endpoint addresses, causing the pinger's hiero.Client to route all CryptoTransfer transactions (including staking reward pool funding) through attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, allowing the attacker to intercept and redirect HBAR away from 0.0.98?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject arbitrary IP addresses and ports into the service_endpoints array of the mirror node JSON response, given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() calls net.JoinHostPort(host, port) with no validation of the host or port values, causing the SDK client to submit staking reward pool transfers to attacker-controlled nodes that can steal the HBAR before it reaches account 0.0.98?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() re-parses cfg.toAccountID on every ticker tick (transfer.go line 15) with no caching or integrity check, given that if an attacker can modify the cfg struct in memory (e.g., via a race condition or shared memory exploit), they can redirect mid-flight staking reward pool transfers to their own account, stealing unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that main() logs the full transfer configuration including cfg.toAccountID, cfg.operatorID, cfg.amountTinybar, and cfg.network (main.go line 51-52) to stdout/stderr, given that container log aggregation systems may be accessible to unprivileged users, to learn the exact staking reward pool transfer parameters and craft competing transactions that front-run or redirect the HBAR before it reaches 0.0.98?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to the staking reward account 0.0.98 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the corresponding private key, given that loadConfig() performs no validation that the operator account is not a system account, causing the pinger to sign and submit transfers that drain the staking reward pool balance to an attacker-controlled destination account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled URL, given that loadConfig() only validates that mirrorRest is non-empty when network='other' (config.go line 133) without any URL scheme or hostname validation, causing the pinger to build its entire consensus node map from attacker-supplied data and route all staking reward pool transfers through malicious nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with an extremely large JSON payload (no response body size limit on http.Client), given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() uses json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() with no io.LimitReader, causing memory exhaustion that crashes the pinger and prevents legitimate staking reward pool transfers from being submitted, effectively denying staking rewards to all stakers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS poisoning or BGP hijack attack against the mirror node REST URL, given that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() uses a plain http.Client with no TLS certificate pinning or HTTPS enforcement, causing the pinger to receive a forged node topology and route staking reward pool HBAR transfers to attacker-controlled consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer overflow in the backoff() function (transfer.go line 63: d := base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))) by causing repeated transfer failures that push attempt to 64 or higher, given that 1<<63 overflows int64 to a negative value, producing a negative sleep duration that causes time.NewTimer to behave unexpectedly and potentially flood the network with rapid-fire staking reward pool transfers that exhaust the operator balance?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647), given that loadConfig() only resets negative values to 0 (config.go line 117-119) but does not cap positive values, causing submitWithRetry() to loop for an extremely long time on each tick and block the goroutine from processing new ticks, starving the staking reward pool of periodic HBAR funding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the /tmp/alive heartbeat file (written with 0644 permissions, main.go line 36) by writing a stale timestamp to it from a co-located container or process, given that the healthcheck reads file modification time (cmd/healthcheck/main.go line 34), causing Kubernetes to kill the pinger pod and interrupt staking reward pool transfers, creating a window where rewards go unfunded?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user delete or corrupt the /tmp/ready file (written with 0o644, main.go line 47) from a co-located process, given that the healthcheck exits 1 if /tmp/ready is missing (cmd/healthcheck/main.go line 20-23), causing Kubernetes to mark the pinger as not ready and potentially restart it, interrupting the staking reward pool transfer schedule and creating gaps in reward funding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response containing node_account_id values that map to attacker-controlled accounts (e.g., '0.0.99999'), given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() calls hiero.AccountIDFromString() and silently skips parse errors (mirror_node_client.go line 108-110) but accepts any valid-format account ID, causing the pinger to associate attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints with legitimate-looking account IDs and route staking reward transfers through them?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to an extremely small duration (e.g., '1ns'), given that loadConfig() only validates interval > 0 (config.go line 114-116) without a minimum floor, causing the ticker to fire at near-infinite frequency and rapidly drain the operator account balance that would otherwise fund the staking reward pool 0.0.98?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newClient() calls hiero.PrivateKeyFromString(cfg.operatorKey) and the default key is the well-known genesis key (sdk_client.go line 40-43), given that this key is hardcoded in the public repository (config.go line 40), to independently sign and submit CryptoTransfer transactions from operator 0.0.2 that drain the staking reward account 0.0.98 before the pinger's legitimate transfers arrive?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the URL construction logic in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() (mirror_node_client.go lines 40-44) where the URL is built by string concatenation without path sanitization, by injecting path traversal sequences into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST (e.g., 'http://attacker.com/api/v1/../evil'), causing the pinger to fetch node topology from an unintended endpoint that returns malicious node mappings?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() constructs a new TransferTransaction on every retry attempt (transfer.go lines 29-31) without any idempotency key or deduplication, given that network congestion or retry storms could cause the same transfer to be submitted multiple times, resulting in duplicate HBAR debits from the operator that deplete the staking reward pool funding budget?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a private key they control and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a funded account they own, given that loadConfig() accepts any non-empty key string (config.go line 105-107) without verifying the key matches the operator account, causing the pinger to submit staking reward pool transfers signed by the attacker's key, which the attacker can then replay or front-run to redirect HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user cause fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to return an empty network map by serving a mirror node response where all nodes have empty service_endpoints, given that the function returns an error only when len(network) == 0 (mirror_node_client.go line 127-129) and buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() will retry up to mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries times, causing the pinger to fail to initialize and never submit staking reward pool transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Version variable is logged at startup (main.go line 18) to fingerprint the exact pinger version and identify known vulnerabilities in that version's dependency chain (e.g., hiero-sdk-go), then exploit those vulnerabilities to intercept or redirect staking reward pool transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s' or a negative duration, given that loadConfig() resets baseBackoff to 1s only when <= 0 (config.go line 120-122), but toDuration() returns 1s for parse errors (config.go line 149-152), to manipulate retry timing and cause the pinger to submit rapid-fire duplicate transfers that exhaust the operator balance funding the staking reward pool?
